title: digitaria ischaemum var. lasiophylla
intro: Digitaria ischaemum var. lasiophylla, also known as Dallis grass or button grass, is a type of invasive grass native to Africa and Asia that has been naturalized in many parts of the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Digitaria ischaemum var. lasiophylla include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
